channel.title: McKinsey Insights & Publications
channel.copyright: Copyright (c) McKinsey & Company. All rights reserved. Reprinted by permission.
channel.generator: Sitecore CMS: http://www.sitecore.net. Sitecore RSS module: McKinsey.RSS, Version=1.3.2.11937, Culture=neutral, PublicKeyToken=null

item.title: Mid-year update: Five surprises from China’s consumer market
item.description: Although macro indicators remain mixed and headwinds persist, several lesser-known developments are signaling deeper shifts in how China consumes, innovates, and competes both at home and on the global stage.

item.title: What’s trending in white goods? Durable, efficient, and affordable
item.description: How can OEMs navigate frugal consumers and shifting macroeconomics in the increasingly competitive white goods sector? We examine our latest global state of the industry customer survey to learn more.

item.title: The McKinsey Crossword: Human-Centric Leadership | No. 245
item.description: Sharpen your problem-solving skills the McKinsey way, with our weekly crossword. Each puzzle is created with the McKinsey audience in mind, and includes a subtle (and sometimes not-so-subtle) business theme for you to find. Answers that are directionally correct may not cut it if you’re looking for a quick win.

item.title: How Uday Kotak uses dreams to manage risk and build value
item.description: Founder and director of Kotak Mahindra Bank, Uday Kotak, believes that common sense, an open mind, and strategic partnerships help build an institution that lasts beyond the individual.

item.title: Mapping the US affordable housing crisis and unlocking opportunities for economic mobility
item.description: Securing rising living standards for all Americans requires action on the affordable housing shortage.

item.title: Digital-led with a human touch: The next era in small-business banking
item.description: To fend off the rise of digital attackers, banks must serve MSMEs better with a model that effectively blends the best of digital and AI with the personal touch of an advisor.

item.title: What is RegTech?
item.description: RegTech, or “regulatory technology,” refers to technologies that improve processes at financial-services organizations, particularly those related to risk and compliance.

item.title: The data center balance: How US states can navigate the opportunities and challenges
item.description: State governments will need to balance a complex range of costs and a full view of the benefits as they weigh their strategies in data center development.

item.title: Rich in resilience: Dealmakers deliver strong first-half results in M&A
item.description: Nerves of steel or “same old, same old”? Either way, dealmakers stared down volatility, leading to higher global M&A activity in the first half of this year.

item.title: How to get your operating model transformation back on track
item.description: “All that work, and what did it get us?” Leaders feel frustrated when transformations fall short, but they can achieve their goals by avoiding six common pitfalls.
item.category: Insights on People and Organizational Performance

item.title: How agentic AI can change the way banks fight financial crime
item.description: Financial institutions are allocating significant resources to fighting financial crime, but they are generally making little progress. AI-based solutions may be an accelerator.

item.title: Author Talks: The five ‘cross-pressures’ that define leadership
item.description: How can leaders learn from disruption and pursue purpose? Stanford lecturer in management Robert E. Siegel shares a systems leadership playbook and transforms classroom lessons into boardroom learnings.

item.title: Slowing data growth: A new telecommunications paradigm?
item.description: Annual mobile-data growth rates are expected to fall from more than 50 percent in 2019 to as low as 10 percent in 2029. Will this lead to unprecedented competition or a new profitable era for telcos?

item.title: Dr. Rupal Malani on the way forward for clinical-care organizations
item.description: McKinsey’s Dr. Rupal Malani discusses changing policies and workforce shortages and how leaders are using M&A and AI to improve margins, operations, and care delivery.

item.title: How Toll transformed procurement into an enterprise value driver
item.description: What does it take to lead a procurement transformation that delivers lasting impact in a world where the function is asked to do everything from managing volatility to generating sustainability and savings?

item.title: The McKinsey Crossword: Rather Iffy | No. 244
item.description: Sharpen your problem-solving skills the McKinsey way, with our weekly crossword. Each puzzle is created with the McKinsey audience in mind, and includes a subtle (and sometimes not-so-subtle) business theme for you to find. Answers that are directionally correct may not cut it if you’re looking for a quick win.

item.title: Manufacturing in rural America: A plan for K–12–industry partnerships
item.description: A seismic threefold increase in advanced-manufacturing investment could transform rural America by driving economic growth, creating high-quality jobs, and revitalizing communities.

item.title: The speed-to-market imperative for life sciences capital delivery
item.description: Life sciences companies are spending big on new capital projects. Industry leaders who embrace capital excellence could secure a competitive edge.

item.title: Small towns, massive opportunity: Unlocking rural America’s potential
item.description: Six strategies could catalyze growth and improve the well-being and economic mobility of rural residents across the country.

item.title: Competitive advantage through cybersecurity: A board-level perspective
item.description: Five leading cybersecurity executives and public company directors discuss how chief information security officers and boards can work together to grow and protect their organizations.

item.title: Scaling bigger, faster, cheaper data centers with smarter designs
item.description: Powering AI and new computation technologies can accelerate data center project delivery and optimize spending to reduce the projected $1.7 trillion global spend through 2030 by up to $250 billion.

item.title: Rewiring pharma’s regulatory submissions with AI and zero-based design
item.description: Six building blocks could help pharma organizations slash filing timelines from months to weeks, potentially unlocking as much as $180 million in net present value for priority assets.

item.title: Intelligence at scale: Data monetization in the age of gen AI
item.description: By harnessing gen AI, companies can leapfrog from selling data to building robust data products that deliver actionable intelligence and unlock previously untapped value.

item.title: Author Talks: What does it take to build and scale a start-up?
item.description: Creating a successful business venture is more than just a notion. An executive coach outlines the risks and rewards of business building and shares the core elements of successful start-ups.

item.title: A conversation about valuation
item.description: Tim Koller shares insights from the eighth edition of Valuation, reflects on economic trends, and considers the lasting influence of Warren Buffett.

item.title: Rethinking the future: A new operating model for gas distributor success
item.description: A new target operating model is crucial if gas distribution system operators are to capture the opportunities presented by changing market forces.

item.title: Looking under the hood: A new approach to mobility investing?
item.description: As overall investment trends in future mobility have remained steady, analysis reveals that the market is undergoing deeper structural change.

item.title: Author Talks: Why well-being intelligence should be a leadership priority
item.description: How can organizations move from reactive mental health efforts to proactive well-being strategies? A Cambridge professor shares a blueprint for supporting teams from the inside out.

item.title: Rewriting the robotics playbook: From promise to performance
item.description: The field of robotics presents opportunities in industries from manufacturing to healthcare to agriculture. Researchers are exploring the ethical and safety implications to set safe guardrails.

item.title: Discussing the future of AI-powered personalization
item.description: At Cannes Lions, McKinsey partner Eli Stein and Jasper CMO Loreal Lynch explore unlocking AI value at scale and building personalization that performs.

item.title: How the right operating model can fix a strategy that falls short
item.description: Stop tinkering with your org chart. Start rethinking your operating model—and closing the gap between strategy and performance as a result.
item.category: Insights on People and Organizational Performance

item.title: Guardrails for growth: Building a resilient payments system
item.description: Facing a complex array of threats, payments providers will need to embrace a mix of traditional and emerging approaches.

item.title: Global Economics Intelligence executive summary, June 2025
item.description: Overall economic volatility has subsided, but consumer confidence is deteriorating and demand remains fragile, despite inflation decelerating across most countries.

item.title: Unlocking Switzerland’s potential: The rise of online marketplaces
item.description: Thanks to a confluence of factors, Swiss online platforms could be positioned for accelerated growth.

item.title: What is a data center?
item.description: A data center is a facility that houses and runs IT infrastructure that’s critical to the digital economy, particularly gen AI.

item.title: Reinventing apparel manufacturing for speed, growth, and sustainability
item.description: Emerging trends, shifting demands, and growing supply chain complexity are reshaping the landscape for apparel manufacturers. McKinsey partner Javier del Pozo shares his thoughts on moving forward.

item.title: The McKinsey Crossword: Improving Efficiency and Effectiveness | No. 243
item.description: Sharpen your problem-solving skills the McKinsey way, with our weekly crossword. Each puzzle is created with the McKinsey audience in mind, and includes a subtle (and sometimes not-so-subtle) business theme for you to find. Answers that are directionally correct may not cut it if you’re looking for a quick win.

item.title: How public-company boards can thrive by adopting private equity practices
item.description: Public-company boards can boost their effectiveness by embracing key strategies from the private equity playbook to enhance value creation and long-term impact.

item.title: Five big tests for Australia’s productivity agenda
item.description: Australia’s economic engine is stalling. To reignite growth, new initiatives must pass five tests for how effectively they accelerate productivity. The first test is unlocking investment.

item.title: How a global biopharma is adopting an end-to-end AI transformation
item.description: Business leaders are using AI and agentic AI to create value despite unprecedented change. A pioneer’s journey offers many lessons.

item.title: A leap in automation: The new technology behind general-purpose robots
item.description: As hardware and software advance, general-purpose robots may evolve from a niche curiosity to an integral part of the workplace.

item.title: Supporting Ohio’s ambition to be the “best place to age in the nation”
item.description: The Ohio Department of Aging partnered with McKinsey to help expand access and improve quality of care and services for older adults and their families across the state.

item.title: Unlocking US federal permitting: A sustainable growth imperative
item.description: The permitting process for large infrastructure projects is costly and time consuming. Accelerating timelines could help meet critical infrastructure needs and fuel future growth.

item.title: Here’s how budgets can keep up with accelerating uncertainty
item.description: As CFOs reflect on budget seasons that come and go too quickly, it’s time to recognize the advantages of a more agile, proactive, and sophisticated budgeting process.

item.title: Don’t delegate the AI revolution: A conversation with Sanofi CEO Paul Hudson
item.description: The global biopharmaceutical company is using AI and agentic AI to accelerate how it chases breakthroughs in medicine.

item.title: Sustainability in packaging: What do European consumers value in 2025?
item.description: Our survey of European consumers’ attitudes toward sustainable packaging shows shifting expectations and regional nuances in a market that generally values both sustainability and affordability.

item.title: Rooted in community, poised for growth: Unlocking San Antonio’s potential
item.description: San Antonio’s growth is fueled by collaboration, resilience, and opportunity. With bold investments and a unified vision, the region can elevate its industries, talent, and global profile.

item.title: The cost of (un)doing business
item.description: In a divestiture, companies must overcome the complexities of disentanglement.

item.title: Powering the energy transition’s motor: Circular rare earth elements
item.description: Demand for rare earth elements needed for magnets will likely surge over the next ten years. Postconsumer scrap could unlock additional supply, although recovery remains a challenge.

item.title: Talent, tech, and team: The formula for medtech R&D success
item.description: Attracting and retaining new talent and fostering collaboration are critical new demands on medtech R&D. Insights from top executives reveal the skills, structures, and cultures needed to win.
